titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Lois Ames Collection of Sylvia Plath
titlestmt.author: Elise Riley

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 14 published digital objects.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday June 23, 2026 at 23:00

did.unittitle: Lois Ames collection of Sylvia Plath

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Correspondence, writings, printed material, research material, photographs, and other papers compiled by Lois Ames in order to write a biography of Sylvia Plath.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 12-13 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James Cummins Bookseller on the George B. Alvord Fund, Danford N. Barney Jr. Fund, and Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 2025.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is organized into three series: I. Correspondence, 1966-1989; II. Research Material, 1950-2001; and III. Audiovisual material, 1971-1982.

bioghist.head: LOIS AMES (1931-2022)
bioghist.p: Lois (neé Sisson) Ames (1931-2022) was a writer, poet, therapist, and social worker. She grew up in Massachusetts and graduated from Smith College with a bachelor's degree in English. She began writing when she moved to New York City after graduation and later enrolled in a social work program at the University of Chicago before marrying Robert Webb Ames. Ames continued to study social work and psychotherapy and became friends with Anne Sexton during a poetry workshop at Bread Loaf in Vermont. Ted Hughes named her as Sylvia Plath's first official biographer, and she spent years corresponding with Hughes and other people in Plath's life to research for the book which was ultimately never published. Ames wrote the biographical note for Plath in the American publication of The Bell Jar . She also co-edited Anne Sexton: A Self Portrait in Letters with Anne's daughter, Linda Gray Sexton, after Sexton's death. In her later life, Ames returned to Massachusetts where she practiced psychotherapy. She died in 2022.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Lois Ames collection of Sylvia Plath. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Yale Archival Processing Manual.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Plath Family papers (YCAL MSS 1369); Janet Malcolm papers (YCAL MSS 935).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains correspondence, photographs, writings, printed materials, and audiovisual materials created by or compiled by Lois Ames for a biography of Sylvia Plath. The bulk of the material was created and assembled during the decade after Plath's death in 1963 when Ames conducted her research.

The correspondence, notes, and audiovisual material capture conversations and interviews intended to deepen Ames's understanding of Plath's life. Included in the collection are materials created by Plath herself such as correspondence to Herbert Hitchen and progress reports for her novel The Bell Jar , which she submitted for the Saxton Fellowship. Also present in the collection are writings, periodicals, and books by or about Sylvia Plath, and her ex-husband, Ted Hughes.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Lois Ames collection of Sylvia Plath is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence files from Lois Ames's research to write a biography for Sylvia Plath which was ultimately never published. Correspondents include Plath family members, friends, publishers, and other Plath researchers.

Because the materials in this series maintain the original order created by Ames, the files contain a mix of incoming, outgoing, carbons, and drafts of correspondence. Of note among the correspondents are Sylvia's mother, Aurelia Plath, Ted Hughes, and Olwyn Hughes with whom Ames wrote to frequently about the content and progress of the biography.
Also included in this series is third party correspondence from Sylvia Plath.

did.unittitle: Sylvia Plath correspondence to Herbert Hitchen
Copies of letters from Sylvia Plath to Anne Sexton

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series are arranged into six subseries: Notes and files; Sylvia Plath materials; Writings; Periodicals; Books; and Ephemera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains research material compiled by Lois Ames in the process of writing a biography of Sylvia Plath that was ultimately never published. Materials include notes, clippings, originals drafts of Sylvia Plath's work, writings about Sylvia Plath, books, periodicals, and ephemera.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this subseries are arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains notes, notebooks, contact information, and other materials related to Lois Ames's research into Sylvia Plath's life in order to write a biography. Included in the material are Ames's requests and receipts for funding received to work on the biography project and as well as an outline for her research. Also included are written notes from conversations and interviews that Ames conducted.

did.unittitle: Materials from Sylvia Plath: Local Roots, Legendary Writer event

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this subseries are arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains materials created by or related to Sylvia Plath collected by Lois Ames while working on a biography about Plath that was ultimately not published. Included in the materials are photographs of Plath and her family members and copies of her journal and correspondence with Gordon Lameyer. Also included in this subseries are the materials Plath submitted for the Eugene F. Saxton Fellow which feature progress reports for The Bell Jar .

did.unittitle: Copy of Sylvia Plath's thesis on Dostoevsky
Xerox of Gordon Lameyer's letters from 1953
Xerox of Gordon Lameyer's letters from 1954
Xerox of Gordon Lameyer's letters from 1955-1956

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this subseries are arranged alphabetically by author.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains copies of writings by or about Sylvia Plath that Lois Ames collected while researching Plath's life.

did.unittitle: Draft and notes on Plath by Lois Ames
"Toward a Life: Sylvia Plath" by Lois Ames
"The Stress of Living, The Stress of Dying" by Lois Ames
"To ReCollect: A Memoir for Retrieval, Preservation, Reflection, and Instruction" by Lois Ames
"Notes on the Chronological Order of Sylvia Plath's Poems" by Ted Hughes
"The Art of Sylvia Plath" by Charles Newman
Series of poems by Sylvia Plath
"A Closer Look at Ariel, Sylvia Plath: A Memoir, 1954-1955" by Nancy Hunter Steiner
"The Poetry of Sylvia Plath" by Helen Vendler
"The Schizoid Personality as Exemplified by the Life and Work of Sylvia Plath" by Judy Whiteley

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this subseries are arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains periodicals which contain published writings of Sylvia Plath, Ted Hughes, and their contemporaries that Lois Ames collected while researching Plath's life.

did.unittitle: Poetry at the Mermaid souvenir programme
Handbook of the Cheltenham Festival of Literature
Festival of Poetry at the Royal Court Theatre booklet
Critical Quarterly Poetry Supplement number 5, New Poetry
Critical Quarterly Poetry Supplement number 6, American Poetry
Critical Quarterly Poetry Supplement number 7, New Poems

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this subseries are arranged alphabetically by author.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains books written by Sylvia Plath, Ted Hughes, and their contemporaries which Lois Ames collected while researching Sylvia Plath's life. Also included are books of poetry written by Sylvia and Ted's daughter, Frieda Hughes, as well as fine press editions of Plath and Hughes's works.

did.unittitle: Sylvia Plath, method and madness by Edward Butscher
Poems by Ruth Fainlight, Ted Hughes, and Alan Sillitoe
Sylvia Plath: A Dramatic Portrait by Barry Kyle
The Hawk in the Rain by Ted Hughes
The Bell Jar by Sylvia Plath
Crystal Gazer and Other Poems by Sylvia Plath
A Closer Look at Ariel: A Memory of Sylvia Plath by Nancy Hunter Steiner

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this subseries are arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains ephemera Lois Ames collected while researching Sylvia Plath's life. Included in the materials are yearbooks from the schools Sylvia attended; Wellesley High School and Smith College.

did.unittitle: The Wellesleyan , Wellesley High School yearbook
The Wellesleyan , Wellesley High School yearbook

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series are arranged first by carrier type and then alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contain audiovisual material created by or collected by Lois Ames while researching Sylvia Plath's life. Included in the materials are interviews Ames conducted with friends, family members, and colleagues of Sylvia Plath such as her mother, Aurelia Plath, her ex-boyfriend, Gordon Lameyer, and fellow poet, Ruth Fainlight.

did.materialspec: No audio at times. Audio distortion, hiss, and hum are present.

descriptivenote.p: [Aviary] Crockett Alfred Young Fisher Anne Sexton undated

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material: Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Ted Hughes / Richard Murphy / Boston College
did.materialspec: Distortion and static are present. An echo can be heard throughout the tape. The program is in silence from 00:38:08-00:39:00. Speed fluctuations are present at 00:39:00.
Distortion and static are present. Audio, at times, sounds hollow or unclear. There is a buzz in the audio. High background noise is present. A hum can be heard. Interference is also present.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material: Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: Distortion and static can be heard. There is a buzz in the audio. Audio, at times, sounds hollow or unclear. Gradual speed increases are present from 00:02:25 until the end of the tape. The volume gradually decreases from 00:11:59-00:19:37. The program was recorded at a very low level from 00:19:37 until the end. There is a hiss in the program.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material: Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: Distortion, buzz, and static are present. Dropouts in the audio are present. Audio, at times, sounds hollow or unclear. High background noise is present.
